cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-18T00:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-02-17T16:06Z. The source of this event is slight dimming and restructuring of field lines seen located at approx. S50E50 in SDO AIA 171/193 beginning at approx. 2026-02-17T23:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SSW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-02-18T01:25Z. This CME is also seen to the SW in SOHO LSCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. It is overtaken by the much larger, faster CME: 2026-02-18T04:48Z early in the FOV of SOHO LASCO C3. It is also obscured by Earth-shine in GOES CCOR-1.  The source of this CME is most likely a filament eruption near the SW limb. The filament is best seen in SDO AIA 304 and can be seen starting to rise as early as 2026-02-17T16:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Narrow, jet-like CME first seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-02-18T03:48Z. This CME is also seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. No independent source candidate has been found for this CME, and it is possibly outflow following streamer blowout CME: 2026-02-17T11:36Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. No source candidate has been found for this CME.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-02-18T04:48Z. This CME is also seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is most likely related to a large filament eruption near former AR 14371. Filamentary material can be seen erupting beyond the SW limb in SDO AIA 304 beginning around 2026-02-18T04:10Z. A large opening of field lines can also be seen beyond the SW limb in SDO AIA 171 and SDO AIA 193. The same eruption can be more clearly seen in STEREO A EUVI 195 starting around 2026-02-18T03:55Z and later around 2026-02-18T04:45Z in STEREO A EUVI 304 after resumption of data after a real-time data gap.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit of the leading edge in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. There is some uncertainty in these parameters as longitudes ranging from ~W115 to ~W131 yield reasonable measurements.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-02-18T09:24Z. This CME is also seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. This feature is most likely outflow following streamer blowout CME: 2026-02-17T11:36Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Faint, irregularly shaped front seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1, overlapping with the continuous outflows following CME: 2026-02-18T04:48Z. The source could be a very slowly developing eruption of a filament centered around (S25W100) that is seen in STEREO A EUV 304 going for hours after 2026-02-18T07Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis based on the fit of this front in three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1).

cmes.note: A slow and faint minor CME overlapping with the outflow after CME: 2026-02-18T04:48Z, seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2, and possibly super faintly in C3 and GOES CCOR-1. There is no clear source eruption seen in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis based on the fit of this narrow front with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2). No clear source has been found in the EUV imagery to confirm this analysis.

cmes.note: Bright CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-18T19:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-02-18T15:53Z. The source of this event is a filament eruption seen beyond the NE limb in GOES SUVI 304 beginning at approx. 2026-02-18T18:12Z. Field line opening is also visible in GOES SUVI 195 and 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using longitude of E90 due to source beyond the limb and lack of STEREO A COR2 imagery to triangulate.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the East by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-18T20:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-02-18T15:53Z. The source of this event is a long duration C2.2 flare located as approx. (S07E60) which peaked at 2026-02-18T20:42 as seen in GOES SUVI 131. Field line opening is also visible in GOES SUVI 171 and 193 exhibiting a possible Northern deflection. This CME partially overlaps with CME: 2026-02-18T21:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the East by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-18T21:00Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-02-18T15:53Z. The source of this event is field line opening seen from beyond the East limb in GOES SUVI 284 beginning at 2026-02-18T20:27Z. This event partially overlaps with CME: 2026-02-18T20:36Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky using a longitude of E90 due to source from beyond the East limb and lack of STEREO A COR2 imagery to triangulate.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap. The source is likely related to an eruption from Active Region 14374 (N08W58) starting around 2026-02-19T10:00Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery during this event, a stereoscopic measurement was not available. The measured parameters may therefore vary as a result.

cmes.note: Narrow, faint, fast, jet-like CME seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2. Covered by the night time data gap in STEREO A. Its source could be the minor ejecta close to the SE limb seen after 2026-02-19T18:50Z in SDO AIA 304, accompanied by the movement/opening of field lines on the SE limb seen in AIA SDO 193/171. Following this, there is row of post-eruptive arcades seen very close to the SE limb (S27E80) in SDO AIA 304.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is covered by a night time data gap in STEREO A. The source of this CME is not clear. So only a plane-of-sky analysis with SOHO LASCO imagery is possible.

cmes.note: A narrow, faint, fast, jet-like CME seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2. Covered by the night time data gap in STEREO A. The source is not clear, as there is a lot of activity close/on/behind the SE limb: it could be a minor ejecta close to the SE limb seen after 2026-02-19T18:50Z in SDO AIA 304, although it is too early. There is also a movement/opening of field lines on the SE limb seen in AIA SDO 193/171 after 2026-02-19T20:30Z, following which there is a row of post-eruptive arcades seen very close to the SE limb (S27E80) in SDO AIA 304.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is covered by a night time data gap in STEREO A. The source of this CME is not clear. So only a plane-of-sky analysis with SOHO LASCO imagery is possible.

cmes.note: Narrow CME seen to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. Not visible in STEREO A COR2. The source may be an ejecta of material beyond the NNW limb in GOES SUVI 304 imagery at 2026-02-20T04:44Z. The eruption is also visible on the limb in SDO AIA 171 imagery in ISWA. The source is not visible in STEREO A EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane of sky measurement using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the N/NE in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1. It is not visible in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap. The source of this CME is visible in SDO/AIA 171 and GOES SUVI 304 to the N/NE beyond the limb starting around 2026-02-21T21:10Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. An extremely faint shock may be seen as a partial southern halo in STEREO A imagery as well. The source is likely far-sided as there are no source signatures on the Earth-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. Due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

cmes.note: Lightbulb shaped CME seen to the SE in all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). It is, save a couple of early images, largely covered by the nighttime data gap in the STEREO A COR2 coronagraph. Its source is a large filament eruption centered near S25E60 which begins to erupt around 2026-02-22T15:00Z, as seen in SDO AIA and GOES SUVI 304, with dimming and post-eruptive arcades seen in SDO AIA 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis based on the later timestamps of GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C3 coronagraphs and fitting the early timestamps of STEREO A COR2. The CME is seen speeding up in later timestamps.

cmes.note: Faint CME with a fuzzy front seen to the W in GOES CCOR-1. The CME is fully covered by the night time data gap in STEREO A COR2. Its source is a C1.5 flare and associated eruption near the southwest limb (~S20W80) which begins to fully lift off around 2026-02-22T16:20Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery. A subsequent opening of field lines is observed over the southwest limb in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/193/284 imagery at this time as well.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis with one-coronagraph (GOES CCOR-1) method, based on the location of the source eruption. The CME is not seen in SOHO LASCO coronagraph because of the scheduled data gap that has not yet backfilled and in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph due to the night time data gap.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the SE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-23T05:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is a very minor eruption seen at approx. S30E80 in SDO AIA 304 beginning at approx. 2026-02-23T04:36Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the east in GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap starting at 2026-02-23T13:38Z. The source is likely far-sided as there are no source signatures visible on the Earth-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of -90 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the west/southwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap starting at 2026-02-23T13:38Z. The source may be related to an eruption observed on the southwest limb starting around 2026-02-23T22:30Z as seen in SDO AIA 171/304 and GOES SUVI 284/304 imagery. A subsequent faint opening of field lines is observed over the southwest limb in SDO AIA 171 imagery at this time as well.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measured longitude based on the approximate source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not available. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the north/northeast in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap starting at 2026-02-23T13:38Z. Additionally, this event is not seen in SOHO LASCO C3 in real-time due to a planned gap between downlink periods starting at 2026-02-24T00:24Z. The source is an eruption centered near N30E30 starting around 2026-02-23T23:20Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery. Dimming is observed north of the eruption site in SDO AIA 193 imagery, suggesting possible northern deflection, and post-eruptive arcades begin to form around 2026-02-24T00:30Z as seen in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/195/284 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not available. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.

cmes.note: CME faintly visible to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. The CME is not clearly visible in GOES CCOR-1 or STEREO A COR2. The potential source may be an eruption seen beyond the northwest limb as moving field lines starting around 2026-02-23T23:55Z in SDO AIA 171/193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane of sky measurement using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 running difference imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: CME seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and very faintly to the SE in STEREO A COR2. The potential source is a slow, gradual eruption with dimming seen starting as early as 2026-02-23T23:12Z in SDO AIA 193 and 211 to the SE, spanning approximately S30E60 to S35E40.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as potential source location.

cmes.note: CME visible to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and the SSW in STEREO A COR2. The source can be seen as opening field lines beyond the east limb in SDO AIA 193 and SDO AIA 171 in ISWA. The eruption is not visible for the point of view of STEREO A EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as the source location.

cmes.note: CME seen to the SE in GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3 as well as the SSW in STEREO A COR2. CME: 2026-02-24T10:36Z directly precedes this CME in the coronagraphs, but it is likely a separate eruption. The source eruption is likely beyond the southeast limb as seen in SDO AIA 193 imagery with additional moving field lines and post-eruptive arcades seen starting at 2026-02-24T14:51Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as the potential source location.
